verdict_reasoning: B4GALT1 encodes beta-1,4-galactosyltransferase 1, a type II single-pass transmembrane protein resident primarily in the trans-Golgi network where it performs glycosylation of nascent glycoproteins and glycolipids. However, it has a well-documented dual localization: a substantial fraction is shed as a soluble form into secretions (milk, serum), and critically, a documented cell-surface pool exists on the outer leaflet of the plasma membrane. The TM-anchored form is detectable on the cell surface by flow cytometry and surface labeling, where it participates in cell-cell adhesion and signaling (e.g., binding lactoferrin and other surface ligands). This surface pool is real but secondary to the dominant Golgi-resident pool, making this a classic dual_localization contextual case — the protein has its own TM anchor at the PM but the PM is not its primary compartment.
